exercises.translation: I study for three hours every day.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates a specific time point (like 3 o'clock) — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about duration, not a time point.
wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — it doesn't fit here because three minutes of daily study would be too short to be meaningful.
wrong_choice_explanations.秒: 秒 means seconds — it doesn't fit here because three seconds of study makes no sense in this context.

exercises.translation: The movie was two and a half hours long.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates time points — it doesn't fit here because we're describing duration, not what time the movie was shown.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because movies don't typically last for days.
wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — it doesn't fit here because movies don't last for weeks.

exercises.translation: I worked for eight hours today.

wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — it doesn't fit here because eight minutes of work in a day would be unrealistically short.
wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates specific times — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about duration of work, not the time when work happened.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because you can't work for eight days in one day.

exercises.example_sentence: 宿題に一__かかりました。

wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — while possible, one minute would be too short for typical homework.
wrong_choice_explanations.秒: 秒 means seconds — it doesn't fit here because one second is impossibly short for homework.
wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates time points — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about how long something took, not when it happened.

exercises.translation: I waited at the hospital for three hours.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates specific times — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about duration of waiting, not the time when waiting occurred.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because waiting three days at a hospital would be unusual for a regular visit.
wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — while grammatically possible, three minutes would be unusually short for a hospital wait.

exercises.example_sentence: 飛行機で十二__かかります。
exercises.translation: It takes twelve hours by airplane.

wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — it doesn't fit here because twelve minutes would be impossibly short for an airplane trip worth mentioning.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because commercial flights don't take multiple days.
wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates time points — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about travel duration, not departure or arrival times.

exercises.translation: I watched TV for four hours.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates specific times — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about how long TV was watched, not when it was watched.
wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — while possible, four minutes would be very short for a TV watching session.
wrong_choice_explanations.回: 回 means times/occasions — it doesn't fit here because it would mean watching TV four times, not the duration of watching.

exercises.translation: The meeting continued for one hour.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates time points — it doesn't fit here because we're describing how long the meeting lasted, not when it occurred.
wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — while one minute is grammatically possible, it would be unusually short for a meeting.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because meetings typically don't last for entire days.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates specific times — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about how long cooking took, not when cooking happened.
wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — while two minutes is grammatically possible, it would be too short for most cooking.
wrong_choice_explanations.回: 回 means times/occasions — it doesn't fit here because it doesn't measure duration but frequency.

exercises.translation: I slept for six hours yesterday.

wrong_choice_explanations.時: 時 indicates specific times — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about sleep duration, not the time when sleep occurred.
wrong_choice_explanations.分: 分 means minutes — it doesn't fit here because six minutes of sleep would be far too little.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because you can't sleep for six days in one day (yesterday).
